summary:
The tumor cells in CLL originate from mature B-cells due to their involvement in processes targeted by therapies such as ibrutinib. This aligns with our understanding of how these cancers develop within this cell lineage. \boxed{\text{Mature B-cells}} Agent2 concludes that tumor cells in both chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) and small lymphoblastoid lymphomas (SLL), collectively referred to hereafter simply as "CLL," are derived from mature B-cells based on therapeutic targets specific for those stages of B-cell development. 

Key Points Summarized:

The tumor cells in CLL come from mature B-cells since they engage treatment mechanisms targeting characteristics unique to fully developed B-cells involved in diseases like CLL. 
Thus,
\[ \boxed{\text{mature B-cells}} \]
